behavior_summary: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea runs when an actor/item triggers the fast-area and uses the triggering instance (implicit 'this') to manage items on the same grid-aligned tile.

The routine computes grid-aligned X/Y from the trigger (masking with 0x7E00) and scans nearby items. If it finds items occupying the same masked grid cell that are not already marked (status & 0x0080), it will either destroy them (unless exempt by type) or mark them as a matched egg to later recreate/pop a replacement.

There is special handling to match egg items: it compares an egg identifier (Egg::getEggId) and an egg subtype field (Item::getUnkEggType) against a subtype chosen by the global flag postEarthQuake (0x2F when postEarthQuake == 0, 0x30 when postEarthQuake == 1). When a matching egg candidate is found the script records it and later creates a new item preserving frame/type, pops it at computed coordinates (summing Z/elevations), and ORs status 0x0080 on the created item.

The script preserves visual/frame and type when creating the replacement (Item::getFrame, Item::create), and uses Item::pop to place the new instance at combined coordinates computed from the triggering and candidate items.
notable_control_flow: Compute masked/grid-aligned coordinates for the triggering item: local4 = Item::getX(this) & 0x7E00, local5 = Item::getY(this) & 0x7E00.
Iterate candidate items; for each candidate: read status (Item::getStatus) and skip if (status & 0x0080) is set.

Compare candidate masked coords (local6/local7) to trigger masked coords (local4/local5). If they match, either: destroy the candidate (Item::destroy) unless candidate type is 0x49 or 0x02, or treat the candidate as a matched egg if Egg::getEggId and Item::getUnkEggType match expected values.

When an egg match is recorded, keep a reference (local2) and set a boolean marker (local9). Later, create a new item with Item::getFrame and Item::create, compute final spawn coordinates (combine masked high bits with low bits from candidate), Item::pop the new item, and Item::orStatus with 0x0080 on the created item.
Global flag postEarthQuake gates which egg subtype is valid (two constants observed: 0x2F vs 0x30).
open_questions: What is the exact semantic meaning of the 0x0080 status bit (skip/processed/hidden/temporary)? The code treats it as a guard but the concrete gameplay meaning is not explicit.
What gameplay object types correspond to numeric type codes 0x49 and 0x02 (they are exempt from destruction) and why are they protected?
Is the trigger always an item instance (egg) or can an NPC/avatar enter this area and cause the same behavior? The code uses an implicit 'this' but caller context is not explicit.
Are there other side effects or global flags affected by this function in other contexts (the script reads postEarthQuake but does not write any globals here)?

main_function.name: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: When an item/actor enters this area's trigger, align coordinates to a grid, remove or reconcile conflicting items on that grid cell, detect matching eggs (by Egg::getEggId and a subtype influenced by the postEarthQuake flag), and—if a match is found—create/pop a replacement item preserving frame/type and mark it with status 0x0080.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the fast-area entry event (U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea process type 0x021B).
Operates on the implicit 'this' instance (the triggering item/actor).

function_map.name: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area trigger handler that manages colocated items and egg-matching/respawn behavior

external_calls.purpose: Read world X coordinate of trigger and candidates to compute grid-aligned X (masking with 0x7E00).
Read world Y coordinate of trigger and candidates to compute grid-aligned Y (masking with 0x7E00).
Read elevation/Z to compute spawn elevation when placing a created item (sum of Zs).
Check status bits to skip already-processed/guarded items (tests bit 0x0080).
Read item type to exempt certain types (observed values 0x49 and 0x02) from destruction; also to pass type/frame into created copies.
Remove conflicting items that occupy the same grid cell and are not exempt by type/status.
Read an egg-subtype field to match a specific egg subtype (compared to 0x2F or 0x30 depending on postEarthQuake).
Obtain an egg identifier to match eggs across items (used to find a paired/matching egg).
Obtain visual/frame index from a candidate item so the created/replaced item preserves appearance.
Instantiate a new item (using preserved frame/type) before popping it into the world.
Place the newly created item into the world at the computed X/Y and elevation.
Set status bits (observed OR with 0x0080) on the created item to mark it and avoid reprocessing.

reads.name: postEarthQuake
reads.likely_effect: Selects which egg subtype is considered a valid match (0x2F when clear/0, 0x30 when set/1). This influences which candidate eggs are paired and recreated.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GLOBSWAP::enterFastArea runs when an actor/item triggers the fast-area and uses the triggering instance (implicit 'this') to manage items on the same grid-aligned tile.
